pagination_guide.overview: This guide explains how to configure pagination for REST APIs that return data across multiple pages. The connector supports 5 different pagination strategies commonly used by REST APIs.

when_to_use_pagination.criterion: API returns large datasets split across multiple pages
API documentation mentions pagination, page limits, or cursors
API responses include pagination metadata (next page, total pages, etc.)
You want to fetch ALL data, not just the first page

when_not_to_use_pagination.criterion: API returns all data in a single response
You only need the first page of results

type.name: offset
type.title: Offset-Based Pagination
type.description: Uses numeric offset and limit parameters to paginate through results. Common in SQL-based APIs.
type.how_it_works: - First request: ?offset=0&limit=100 - Second request: ?offset=100&limit=100 - Third request: ?offset=200&limit=100 - Continues until fewer than page_size results returned

type.name: page
type.title: Page-Based Pagination
type.description: Uses page numbers to paginate through results. Most common pagination type.
type.how_it_works: - First request: ?page=1&per_page=50 - Second request: ?page=2&per_page=50 - Third request: ?page=3&per_page=50 - Continues until fewer than page_size results returned

parameters.param: Query parameter name for page number (e.g., "page", "p")
Query parameter name for page size (e.g., "per_page", "limit", "size")
Starting page number (default: 1, some APIs use 0)

type.name: cursor
type.title: Cursor-Based Pagination
type.description: Uses opaque cursor tokens to paginate through results. Best for real-time data and large datasets.

parameters.param: JSON path to next cursor in response (e.g., "pagination.next_cursor", "meta.next")

type.important: The next_cursor_path uses dot notation to navigate nested JSON. Example: "pagination.next_cursor" looks for response.pagination.next_cursor

type.name: link_header
type.title: Link Header Pagination (RFC 5988)
type.description: Uses HTTP Link headers to provide next page URLs. Follows RFC 5988 standard.
type.how_it_works: - First request: /api/data - Response includes header: Link: ; rel="next" - Second request: Uses URL from Link header - Continues until no "next" link in header
type.important: This is the simplest pagination type - the connector automatically extracts the next page URL from the Link header.

type.name: next_url
type.title: Next URL Pagination
type.description: API provides full URL to next page in response body. Common in hypermedia APIs.
type.how_it_works: - First request: /api/data - Response includes: {"data": [...], "pagination": {"next": "https://api.example.com/data?page=2"}} - Second request: Uses URL from response - Continues until next URL is null or missing

parameters.param: JSON path to next URL in response (e.g., "pagination.next", "links.next", "next_page")

type.important: The next_url_path uses dot notation to navigate nested JSON. The URL can be absolute or relative.

configuration_placement.rule: Place it inside the table definition, alongside $path and field definitions
Each table can have its own pagination configuration
If API doesn't use pagination, omit $pagination entirely

example.title: Stripe Charges (Cursor-Based)
example.note: Stripe uses the last item's ID as the cursor. The path "data.-1.id" means: get the last item in the data array, then get its id field.

issue.solution: Verify parameter names match API documentation exactly

possible_causes.cause: Wrong initial_page (should be 1 or 0 depending on API)

issue.solution: Check API documentation for starting page/offset number

possible_causes.cause: page_size too large (API may have maximum limit)

issue.solution: Reduce page_size or add rate limiting handling

best_practices.practice: Always check API documentation for pagination details
Use the largest page_size the API allows for better performance
Test pagination with APIs that have multiple pages of data
For cursor-based pagination, carefully verify the next_cursor_path
For next_url pagination, verify the next_url_path points to the correct field
Consider API rate limits when setting page_size

step.question: Does the API documentation mention pagination?
How does the API indicate the next page?

option.answer: Uses offset and limit (offset=0, offset=100, etc.)
Provides next page URL in Link HTTP header
Provides next page URL in response body
